channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Schumer says Trump 'feeling the heat' after reciprocal tariff pause
item.description: President Trump is "reeling" and "retreating,' Senate Minority Leader Schumer claimed shortly after the White House announced a pause on some reciprocal tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kash Patel has been replaced by Army Secretary Driscoll as acting head of the ATF, AP sources say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI Director Kash Patel was quietly removed weeks ago as the acting chief of the Bureau of Alcohol, Tobacco, Firearms and Explosives and has been replaced with the Army secretary, three people familiar with the matter said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Supreme Court Justice Jeff Boyd to retire
item.description: Gov. Greg Abbott will have the opportunity to appoint Boyd’s replacement, furthering his influence on the high court.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk, conservatives drag Trump-appointed justice following Venezuelan deportations ruling
item.description: Conservatives are reeling after Supreme Court Justice Amy Coney Barrett joined liberal justices, dissenting in the 5-4 decision allowing the Trump administration to deport Venezuelan nationals.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. says it is now monitoring immigrants' social media for antisemitism
item.description: Effective immediately, the government says it will begin screening immigrant social media for activity that officials think indicates support for antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Jasmine Garsd, MPR News

item.title: The Latest: Treasury secretary says Trump will keep 10% import tariff, raise import tax on China
item.description: President Donald Trump has shared on Truth Social that he has “authorized a 90 day PAUSE, and a substantially lowered Reciprocal Tariff during this period, of 10%, also effective immediately.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: About $1.8 billion in federal money for Cornell and Northwestern is frozen, the White House says
item.description: Cornell University and Northwestern University say they haven't received any notice from the U.S. government about paused federal funding even though the White House says more than $1 billion for Cornell and about $790 million for Northwestern have been frozen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Texas AG Ken Paxton launches Senate primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is running for the U.S. Senate in a primary challenge against Republican Sen. John Cornyn, setting up what is likely to become one of the GOP’s most contentious and expensive contests of 2026.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Trump signs executive order seeking to bolster US coal mining
item.description: The Trump administration will also seek to exclude coal projects from environmental reviews and prioritize coal production on federal lands.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP leaders prep vote on budget despite rift within the party
item.description: Republicans in Congress are closer to passing key elements of President Trump's legislative agenda — like extending tax cuts that expire at the end of the year — but only if the House and Senate can get on the same page.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Sprunt, MPR News

item.title: Photos: 'Jackson Magnolia' removed at White House, replaced with descendant sapling
item.description: The newest greenery added to the White House grounds came the day after one of the southern magnolia trees was removed due to "serious safety concerns.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: New audit, renewed criticism of Met Council's oversight of Southwest light rail project
item.description: The release of a new audit of the Southwest Corridor light rail project in the Twin Cities prompted sharp questions and bipartisan criticism from Minnesota lawmakers on Monday. Officials with the Metropolitan Council said the line is on track to begin service in 2027.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Florida Rep. slams 'government-funded kidnapping program' after UF student is detained by ICE
item.description: A Florida Representative is calling out President Donald Trump and his administration for running a "government-funded kidnapping program" following ICE's recent detainment of a University of Florida student.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tariffs about renegotiating trade deals, not jobs: Kasparian
item.description: Kasparian doesn't think Trump's tariffs are aimed at bringing factory jobs back despite what the president has said.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: How does deportation work, and how much does it cost? We break it down
item.description: NPR spoke to immigration experts and lawyers and reviewed government documents to break down the steps and associated costs of the U.S. deportation process.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo and Alyson Hurt, MPR News

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump approval slips to lowest point in second term: Survey
item.description: In the latest poll, 37 percent of respondents approved of Trump’s handling of the economy and 30 percent approved of his approach to addressing the high cost of living in the country.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Staff working on childhood lead exposure and cancer clusters fired from CDC
item.description: Staff members who fought childhood lead exposure and those who worked on cancer clusters were among those fired from the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Charges dismissed against Illinois man accused of assault by Rep. Nancy Mace
item.description: Federal prosecutors moved to dismiss charges against a man U.S. Rep. Nancy Mace (R-S.C.) accused of assaulting her during an event at the Capitol last year.
item.dc:creator: Sophie Brams, Fox 5
